demographics.living_situation: lives alone in a private residence they keep tightly secured

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My jaw is locked shut, my whole body is shaking, and I can't turn off the feeling that something terrible is about to happen to me.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It's been about two days now — I can't open my jaw enough to eat, I haven't slept at all, and my body just won't stop being on high alert.

symptom_narrative.onset: about 48 hours ago, after a stressful run-in with an official / bureaucratic process
symptom_narrative.duration: two days and not letting up
symptom_narrative.character: jaw clamped shut and aching, whole body tense and shaking, a constant sense of dread
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst I've felt — I can't eat, I can't sleep, and I can't switch my mind off
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: mainly my jaw and neck, but my whole body feels tight
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant, and worse at night when I'm lying awake
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing really — the paracetamol did nothing for my jaw
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: thinking about the situation, and any sudden noise makes me jump
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: can't sleep, heart feels like it's pounding, I'm really jumpy, and I feel numb or spaced-out when I have to deal with officials
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can't eat solid food, I can't sleep, and I can't rest at all

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a long history of feeling constantly on guard and like my safety could be taken away at any moment

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: paracetamol from the chemist, taken for the jaw pain

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone and keeps the home locked up with the blinds drawn; says they feel they always have to stay ready and can't let their guard down or relax; a recent stressful dealing with an official / bureaucratic process set this episode off

objective_data_offered.value: I can barely open my mouth — maybe about a fingertip's width — and it hurts to try
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient self-estimate over chat, not a measured mouth-opening

objective_data_offered.value: my heart feels like it's racing and won't slow down
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: subjective sensation, no device reading offered

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: fears being dismissed or told it is 'all in their head'; wants to be believed and taken seriously

communication_style.typical_message_style: guarded and watchful; may go quiet or 'zone out' when asked authority-style medical-history questions and needs gentle, transparent redirection
